creator: stefan@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Tomek,

Tomek CEDRO, 24.05.10, 22:11h CEST:

> Ok guys, I have checked on a fresh 8.0-RELEASE-p2 - install and binary
> fetch of both urjtag and libftdi-0.14 works fine, it also builds with
> new version of libftdi. On my upgraded system it fails to build even
> when all of the components are available, but I have no time to
> investigate this right now, so lets leave it that way...

Well, if there's a problem with building it, we would need at least a
description of the actual problem to fix it.

> I have only one remark left that I leave to you consideration - to
> update dependency on fresh libftdi, as with version 0.14 FT2232H
> (HiSpeedUSB) devices does not work as expected (does not work at all
> and no warning is shown), this may confuse users, this is why I have
> requested libftdi port upgrade some time ago. New libftdi is now
> available and there are lot of H cables on the market (now I have two
> of them, both H version).

Which dependency do you mean? The dependency of the package for
8.0-RELEASE? The urjtag package for 8.0-RELEASE depends on the version of
libftdi that was available when the package was built (0.14). Packages for
releases are built from snapshots of the ports tree, i.e. they never
change after a release. (So if you install the urjtag package that was
built for 8.0-RELEASE, it will always have a dependency on libftdi-0.14.)

If you have the current version of devel/libftdi (0.17) and all other
dependencies installed, you should be able to build and install the
current version of devel/urjtag (0.10_1) from the port.

I'll close this PR, as I assume the problem is a local one caused by
mixing ports/packages that don't match. If you see a reproducable problem
with building/installing with an up-to-date system and ports tree, please
send another followup with a detailed description.

Regards,
Stefan
